data.body:
PASS 1 review (rubric T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture). Posting findings + affirmations for the "Document reusable machinery API reference" plan.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1):
- No bright-line for *novel architecture* fires: this is a documentation-only ticket (the plan explicitly states "no behavior changes" / "documentation only"). It introduces no new dependency, external integration, security mechanism, performance target, or migration — it *describes* existing subsystems (`rebar.signing`, `rebar.llm.runner`/`workflow`, `rebar.llm.prompts`, `rebar.llm.findings`), all of which exist in the source tree. No prior-art search for a new pattern is required.
- T1(c) unverified-capability check passes: the plan asserts no "library supports X" capability claims; it documents in-repo APIs and adds an explicit AC ("Each documented signature/return shape is verified against the current source (no drift)").

FINDING (T1(a) — prior art / anti-repackaging):
- [ ] Reconcile the plan with the already-existing `docs/reuse-surface.md` before treating this as net-new work — the doc this ticket proposes to "create" appears to already exist at the exact path the plan names as an example.
  - location: plan "Why" ("there is no single developer-facing API REFERENCE…", "This ticket creates that reference") + AC line 1 ("A developer-facing API reference exists (new doc under `docs/`, e.g. `docs/reuse-surface.md` …)")
  - criteria: T1
  - evidence: `docs/reuse-surface.md` already exists and substantially covers all four scoped sections — §"The signing surface — `rebar.signing`" documents `signing_key`/`key_fingerprint`/`parse_manifest`/`compute_signature`/`sign_manifest`/`verify_signature`/`verify_record`/`head_sha`, the manifest + `PAYLOAD_VERSION=1`/HMAC-SHA256 canonicalisation, the `certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned` verdict table, the head_sha freshness binding, the SIGNATURE last-writer-wins replay, AND a "Worked example — a NEW gate reusing the surface" — i.e. nearly the entire scope/AC of this ticket. The plan's framing ("there is no single … reference", "This ticket creates that reference") does not acknowledge this prior art.
  - suggested_fix: Re-scope the ticket from "create" to "verify/extend/de-duplicate": (1) state that `docs/reuse-surface.md` already exists and is the target artifact; (2) reframe the work as auditing it against the current source for drift (AC line 7) and filling any genuinely-missing pieces (e.g. confirm the runtime/prompt/output-schema sections are as complete as the signing section); (3) avoid re-authoring content that already exists. If the intent really was net-new, justify why the existing doc is insufficient rather than an edit target.

This is the only T1 finding; the chunk is otherwise clean for this pass.
